name: Cheesecake with Cranberry Cherry Topping
recipeIngredient: 1/2 10 oz. jar red currant jelly, 1/3 cup
16 1/2 ounce can pitted dark cherries, drained
recipeInstructions:
Early in the day or a day ahead: Prepare apricot puree. In a 1 quart saucepan, over Medium-High heat, heat apricots, 1/2 cup sugar and 1/2 cup water to boiling. Cook, stirring occasionally, until all liquid evaporates. Blend the apricot mixture in a food processor with a knife blade attached or mash it in the saucepan with a potato masher. Refrigerate until completely cooled.
Meanwhile, in a 2 quart saucepan, mix cornstarch and currant jelly over Medium heat until blended. Cook the jelly mixture until melted and smooth, stirring occasionally. Stir in cranberries. Heat to boiling. Cook, stirring occasionally until cranberry skins pop, about 4 minutes. Remove from heat. Stir in cherries. Cover and refrigerate until ready to use.
Preheat oven to 350°F. In a 9x3-inch springform pan, mix graham cracker crumbs and butter or margarine with your hands. Press the mixture onto the bottom and up the sides of the pan. Spread the apricot puree evenly over the bottom of the crust in the pan.
In a large bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth. Beat in 1 1/4 cups sugar. Add vanilla and eggs. Beat for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl often with a rubber spatula.
Pour the cream cheese mixture on top of the apricot puree in the pan. Bake the cheesecake for 50-55 minutes until just beginning to brown around the edge. The center may appear loose. Cool the cheesecake in the pan on a wire rack. Cover and refrigerate the cheesecake for at least 6 hours or until well chilled.
To serve, carefully remove the side of the springform pan. Spoon the cranberry-cherry topping over the cheesecake.
